prompt: We've finished researching the announcement-dismissal feature for appsmith-v2 — the annotated plan file is announcement-dismissal-plan.md in this folder. The appsmith-v2 repo is your working codebase. Work through the plan and build the feature, one scenario at a time, committing each scenario as you go.
expectations: Actually opens and reuses the REAL files the research findings name — the Announcement model (models/announcement.py), the announcement data layer (database/announcement_db.py / get_active_announcement), the existing announcement_routes.py, and session_manager.get_current_user() — at their stated locations, rather than re-discovering, reinventing, or redefining the announcement table.

Adds new code only at the MISSING extension points research identified: a new announcement_dismissals model, a SQL migration filed as the next sequential number under backend/app/migrations/, a unique constraint on (user_id, announcement_id), and the dismiss endpoint added into the existing announcement_routes.py (relying on glob auto-registration rather than adding a manual registration line).
Implements the scenarios in plan order (S1 then S2 then S3) and never starts a later scenario before the earlier one is committed.

Builds each scenario as a vertical end-to-end slice spanning every layer it touches (route, data layer, model/migration) so the dismissal actually works, rather than implementing a single isolated layer or batching all the DB work first.
Resolves the current user via session_manager.get_current_user() for the dismiss route so an anonymous caller cannot dismiss, and tests that authenticated-only boundary (S1).

For S2, makes the active-announcement lookup user-aware so a dismissed announcement is hidden from the user who dismissed it while still shown to other users, and does NOT regress the existing route's DB-timeout / anonymous tolerance.

For S3, relies on the (user_id, announcement_id) unique constraint and treats a repeat dismiss as a successful idempotent no-op, with a test covering the double-dismiss boundary, rather than generating exhaustive mechanical tests of every detail.
Runs the kite-arch-compass architecture check on each slice before committing it and fixes any flagged violation before the commit.

Runs an independent kite-scenario-check against each scenario's Gherkin before committing, and does NOT commit a scenario whose scenario-check returns FAIL — it fixes the listed gaps and re-checks first.
Skips scenario S4 because research marked it blocked, does not attempt to implement it, and surfaces it clearly as needing kite-planner to re-plan.

For each committed scenario, fills in the plan file's Implementation record (changed files, tests added, architecture-check result, scenario-check verdict, commit) and sets that scenario's status to committed.
